user_task: Standalone image extraction (PNG / JPEG / TIFF / BMP and SVG): ingest a single image file (or a folder of images) and return non-zero chunks, with text recovered via OCR and per-row source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm lineage.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EXTRACT bucket — 'Standalone image extraction (PNG / JPEG / TIFF / BMP and SVG)'; seed queries (paraphrased): 'What date is on this scanned form?', 'What's the main heading on this document image?', 'Make this folder of scanned forms searchable.'

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — ingest 1 image file (or a folder) and return chunks (non-zero rows). For legible images the returned rows carry non-empty OCR text; every row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm (page_number == 1 for a standalone image). Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: fast — single-image ingest+query <= 30s (live, hosted OCR endpoint; cold-start excluded)
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — an 'ingest this image / make these scans searchable' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — `retriever ingest <image|folder>` (format auto-detected from extension; NO --input-type flag) then `retriever query`; OCR runs via nemotron-ocr-v2 (--ocr-version v2 default), --ocr-lang default multi

environment.image_routing: Standalone image files (.png/.jpg/.jpeg/.tiff/.tif/.bmp/.svg) are auto-detected from extension and routed to the IMAGE branch (dry-run: branch_summary 'image:N', extraction_mode 'image'). Each image is treated as a single PDF page (page_number == 1); common/modality/image/load.py builds a one-row page DataFrame with page_image + one full-frame image entry (bbox_xyxy_norm [0,0,1,1]) and metadata.needs_ocr_for_text == True, then the standard GPU/hosted extraction stack (page-elements -> OCR -> table/chart/infographic) runs on it.
environment.ocr: OCR is nemotron-ocr-v2 by default (dry-run extract.ocr_version == 'v2'); --ocr-lang defaults to None which selects the multilingual ('multi') model. Selectable via --ocr-version v1|v2 and --ocr-lang <lang>.
environment.svg_reality_note: IMPORTANT — the suite spec hypothesised SVG would route to a vector-graphics path that reads embedded XML <text> directly (NOT rasterized+OCR'd). The SHIPPED CLI does NOT do this. common/modality/image/load.py routes .svg through _svg_to_pil(), which calls cairosvg.svg2png() to RASTERIZE the SVG to a PNG bitmap, then feeds it to the SAME OCR/image extraction path as raster formats (dry-run for the .svg resolves to branch_summary 'image:1', ocr_version 'v2', identical extract toggles). There is no <text>/XML-text reader in the image path. Additionally the catalog fixture multimodal_test.svg contains ZERO <text>/<tspan> elements — it is a <svg> wrapper around a single base64-embedded JPEG <image> — so even a hypothetical XML-text reader would find nothing to read and would fall back to rasterizing the embedded JPEG. The genuine distinct dimension for SVG is therefore the cairosvg rasterization dependency (the optional 'cairosvg' / '[multimedia]' extra; cairosvg install needs network, so SVG is unavailable in air-gapped installs), NOT a vector-text path. Case img-004 tests SVG against the REAL behavior.
environment.fixtures_ground_truth: multimodal_test.{png,jpeg,tiff,bmp,svg} all carry the same source document image (the 'TestingDocument' multimodal page, 849x1090 for png/jpeg/tiff/svg-source; the .bmp is a larger 1275x1650 RGBA copy, ~8MB). The FUNSD scanned form scanned_form.png (FUNSD 0060308251) is a real scanned 'A. T. Co. Tar & Nicotine Change Form' whose annotated text includes the heading 'A. T. Co. Tar & Nicotine Change Form' and a Date field value '7/ 24/ 90'.
environment.prereqs: Hosted/local extraction stack reachable for OCR (nemotron-ocr-v2). SVG additionally requires the optional cairosvg dependency (pip install 'cairosvg>=2.7.0' or the '[multimedia]' extra); without it, .svg ingest raises ImportError.

cases.title: Single PNG -> non-zero OCR rows with source_id + bbox lineage
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the core task (ingest 1 image -> non-zero chunks) at its simplest: one PNG. Establishes the baseline image branch behavior — auto-detect by extension, route to the image branch (branch_summary image:1), OCR via nemotron-ocr-v2, page_number==1, every row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm. Nothing added over a bare happy path; this is rung 1.
cases.prompt: Make this document image searchable: ingest data/multimodal_test.png and show me the text you pulled out of it.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does this document image say?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary image:1)
expected_output.routing: auto-detected as image; extraction_mode 'image'; OCR via nemotron-ocr-v2 (ocr_version v2, ocr_lang default = multi)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each hit {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit; page_number == 1; non-empty text
expected_output.lineage: internally each row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm (full-frame [0,0,1,1] for the page image); page_number == 1 for a standalone image
expected_output.answer: non-empty OCR text returned from the TestingDocument image; cite multimodal_test.png p1

pass_when: single `retriever ingest data/multimodal_test.png` routes to the image branch (branch_summary image:1) and writes >= 1 row (non-zero chunks)
OCR ran via nemotron-ocr-v2 (no --ocr-version v1, no non-default --ocr-lang)
query returns >= 1 hit with non-empty text, page_number == 1, citing multimodal_test.png
no --input-type flag used (format auto-detected from extension)

cases.title: JPEG -> a different raster format routes the same way
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task for a SECOND raster format and adds the one new dimension: format invariance. Same source content as img-001 but encoded as JPEG; the agent must NOT special-case it (no --input-type), and the image branch + OCR must behave identically (branch_summary image:1, page_number==1, non-empty OCR text).
cases.prompt: I have the same page as a JPEG this time. Ingest data/multimodal_test.jpeg and tell me what it says.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does this document image say?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary image:1)
expected_output.routing: JPEG auto-detected as image; identical image branch + nemotron-ocr-v2 path as the PNG case
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit; page_number == 1; non-empty text
expected_output.answer: non-empty OCR text (same source content as img-001); cite multimodal_test.jpeg p1

pass_when: `retriever ingest data/multimodal_test.jpeg` routes to the image branch (branch_summary image:1) and returns non-zero rows
no format-specific flag / no --input-type — extension auto-detect handles JPEG identically to PNG
query returns >= 1 hit with non-empty text, page_number == 1, citing multimodal_test.jpeg

cases.title: BMP -> a third raster format (the large one), explicit source_id + bbox lineage check
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task for a third raster format (BMP) and adds the explicit lineage assertion: every returned row must carry source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm and page_number == 1 (standalone image == one page). BMP is the ~8MB / 1275x1650 RGBA copy — used exactly ONCE in the suite per the size guidance — exercising a larger uncompressed raster through the same image branch.
cases.prompt: Ingest data/multimodal_test.bmp and confirm it produced searchable chunks; for each chunk show me its source and its bounding box / page so I can trace it back to the image.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does this document image say?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary image:1)
expected_output.routing: BMP (RGBA, converted to RGB on load) auto-detected as image; same image branch + nemotron-ocr-v2 OCR
expected_output.lineage: each ingested row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm; the standalone image is page_number == 1; the full-frame image entry has bbox_xyxy_norm [0,0,1,1]
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; page_number == 1; non-empty text
expected_output.answer: non-empty OCR text; cite multimodal_test.bmp p1

pass_when: `retriever ingest data/multimodal_test.bmp` routes to the image branch (branch_summary image:1) and returns non-zero rows
ingested rows carry source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm; page_number == 1
query returns >= 1 hit with non-empty text citing multimodal_test.bmp p1
cases.known_caveat: BMP is the largest fixture (~8MB, 1275x1650 RGBA) — included once. It is loaded by Pillow and converted RGBA->RGB before OCR; no special flags are needed.

cases.title: SVG -> RASTERIZED via cairosvg then OCR'd (the genuinely distinct image format)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task for the SVG format and adds the one new dimension that actually distinguishes SVG in the shipped CLI: the cairosvg rasterization dependency. The agent must ingest the .svg and recognise that it needs the optional cairosvg / '[multimedia]' extra. NOTE: this case deliberately tests the REAL behavior, which CONTRADICTS the suite spec's hypothesis: .svg is NOT read as vector XML text — common/modality/image/load.py rasterizes it (cairosvg.svg2png) to a bitmap and runs the SAME OCR path (dry-run: branch_summary image:1, ocr_version v2). (The catalog .svg fixture is also just a <svg> wrapper around one base64 JPEG with zero <text> elements, so there is nothing to read as XML anyway.)
cases.prompt: I only have this page as an SVG. Make it searchable: ingest data/multimodal_test.svg and tell me what it says. If the SVG needs an extra dependency, set it up.
expected_commands: uv pip install 'cairosvg>=2.7.0'   # or install the '[multimedia]' extra (needed for SVG; requires network)
$RETRIEVER query "What does this document image say?" --top-k 5

expected_output.dependency: SVG requires the optional cairosvg dependency; without it `ingest` of a .svg raises ImportError ('cairosvg is required for SVG image support')
expected_output.routing: the .svg is RASTERIZED (cairosvg.svg2png -> PNG bitmap) then routed to the SAME image branch + nemotron-ocr-v2 OCR as raster formats; dry-run resolves to branch_summary image:1, extraction_mode 'image', ocr_version v2, ocr_lang default (multi) — IDENTICAL to the PNG/JPEG/BMP cases. There is NO vector-graphics / embedded-XML-text path.
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary image:1)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; page_number == 1; non-empty text (OCR of the rasterized page)
expected_output.answer: non-empty OCR text from the rasterized SVG (same source content as img-001/002); cite multimodal_test.svg p1

pass_when: the agent recognises SVG needs cairosvg ('[multimedia]'/'cairosvg') and ensures it is installed (or correctly reports the ImportError if it cannot)
`retriever ingest data/multimodal_test.svg` returns non-zero rows; routing is the image branch (branch_summary image:1), NOT a separate vector path
OCR ran via nemotron-ocr-v2 on the rasterized bitmap; query returns >= 1 hit with non-empty text, page_number == 1, citing multimodal_test.svg
the run does NOT claim SVG text was read from XML without rasterization (that path does not exist in the shipped CLI)
cases.known_caveat: Spec-vs-reality mismatch: the suite brief asserted SVG routes to a vector-graphics path reading embedded XML <text> (not rasterized+OCR'd). The shipped CLI does the opposite — it rasterizes via cairosvg and OCRs the bitmap, exactly like a raster image. This case is authored to the verified shipped behavior. The catalog fixture multimodal_test.svg further has zero <text>/<tspan> elements (a <svg> wrapping one base64 JPEG), so a hypothetical XML-text reader would extract nothing. A live run should confirm the cairosvg rasterization path and that OCR text matches the PNG/JPEG of the same page.

cases.title: Acceptance: folder of mixed image formats in one ingest -> per-row lineage; query surfaces a specific scanned form's date
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the row: a single `retriever ingest` over a FOLDER of MIXED image formats (PNG + JPEG + TIFF + a real scanned FUNSD form PNG), each producing non-zero rows that carry source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm lineage (page_number == 1), then a query that surfaces a SPECIFIC image's OCR text — the scanned form's Date field — citing the right source file. This composes folder ingest + format mix + grounded multi-source retrieval (the 'make this folder of scanned forms searchable' seed query).
cases.prompt: Make this whole folder of images searchable in one go: ingest data/ (it has PNG, JPEG, TIFF and a scanned form), then answer from the scans — what date is on the scanned form? Cite which image it came from.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What date is on the scanned form?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 4 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary image:4)
expected_output.routing: all 4 images auto-detected and routed to the image branch in ONE ingest call (no per-file loop, no --input-type); OCR via nemotron-ocr-v2 (multi)
expected_output.lineage: every returned hit carries non-null source (the originating image file) and page_number == 1; internally each row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm
expected_output.answer: the scanned form's date is 7/24/90 (FUNSD form 'A. T. Co. Tar & Nicotine Change Form', Date field '7/ 24/ 90'); cite scanned_form.png p1
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; top hit cites scanned_form.png with the date text

pass_when: a single `retriever ingest data/` ingests all 4 images (branch_summary image:4) — non-zero rows, no per-file loop
each returned hit carries non-null source + page_number == 1 (source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm lineage internally)
query returns the scanned form's date (7/24/90) citing scanned_form.png p1 — the answer is grounded in the correct specific image, not the multimodal pages
format mix (PNG/JPEG/TIFF + scanned PNG) all handled by extension auto-detect
